_meta.methodology: Aggregated metrics across millions of jobs published on hundreds of job sites. Covers 16 major occupations across all 50 US states. Excludes duration-based postings, slot-based advertising, and organic traffic.

market_conditions_2025.summary: Two distinct labor markets emerged: Market A (The Flood) for white-collar/remote roles with exploding application volume, and Market B (The Drought) for frontline/licensed roles with structurally constrained supply.

applicant_growth_nov2022_to_nov2025._note: Application volume per job growth multiplier, Nov 2022 to Nov 2025

median_cpa_by_occupation_2025._note: Median Cost Per Application (USD), long-form/ATS applications (5+ step forms), All US
median_cpa_by_occupation_2025.key_insight: Healthcare roles cost nearly 2x more per application than tech/white-collar roles

cpa_by_application_type._note: CPA variation by application complexity for the same roles
cpa_by_application_type.key_insight: Fewer application pages = more applies. Apply rate drops sharply after 2-page applications.

demand_intensity_per_100_employed._note: Job Postings per 100 Employed Workers in 2025. Higher = more demand pressure relative to available workforce.
demand_intensity_per_100_employed.key_insight: Licensed and frontline roles maintained higher demand intensities despite overall labor market cooling.

market_a_flood.description: White-collar, remote-capable roles with exploding application volume
what_works: AI-powered screening, interview scheduling, and interviewing
Structured application forms with positive friction
Success metrics tied to screening, shortlists, or hires
Process designs that evaluate beyond the first wave
what_fails: Using experience requirements as a volume filter
Treating declining CPA as evidence hiring is easier
Optimizing for speed without quality measurement
market_a_flood.cpa_warning: A $15 CPA means nothing if your team can only evaluate 50 of 200 applications

market_b_drought.description: Frontline, licensed, and location-dependent roles with structurally constrained supply
what_works: Push job advertising and passive candidate targeting
Employer brand investment targeted at specific roles/markets
Mobile-optimized candidate experiences stripped of friction
Retention focus: every employee who stays is one fewer role to fill
what_fails: Expecting volume metrics to improve because macro indicates it should
Waiting for the market to correct (constraints are structural not cyclical)
Applying the same CPA targets used for flooded roles
market_b_drought.scheduling_insight: Modest increases in scheduling flexibility can reduce time to hire by more than 50%

experience_cliff_2025.summary: Entry-level access narrowed as employers used experience thresholds as volume filters, not because work demanded it.
consequences: Inflated external hiring costs (mid-level externals cost more than training new grads)
Overtenured workforce (fewer fresh perspectives, innovation slows)
capability_signals: Skills-based hiring evaluations tied to near-term job performance
Screening criteria based on first 90-day requirements
Structured interviews testing learning velocity and problem solving
Work samples revealing approach to unfamiliar problems

the_big_stay.description: Workers staying in current jobs longer due to economic uncertainty and fewer enticing offers
the_big_stay.impact: Reduced backfill opportunities, especially for junior/entry roles
the_big_stay.employer_cited_reason: Low turnover, not budgets, as primary reason hiring slowed

strategic_questions_for_ta_leaders: Which roles consistently flood you with applicants vs remain supply-constrained?
Are you running one process or two?
How are you measuring hiring funnel efficiency?
Are you measuring CPA as a success metric across all roles?
Where have experience requirements increased as a response to volume?
Do your recruiting metrics reflect fundamentally different hiring environments?

integration_notes.collar_intelligence: Use market_type (flood/drought) to adjust channel strategy: flood roles need screening-optimized channels, drought roles need sourcing/push channels
integration_notes.budget_engine: Apply CPA adjustments: healthcare/licensed roles should budget 2x the CPA of tech/white-collar; use demand_intensity to weight supply-constrained roles higher
integration_notes.trend_engine: Feed applicant_growth_multiplier into dynamic CPC calculations as a demand pressure factor
integration_notes.nova_chat: Reference two-market framework when advising on hiring strategy; cite Joveo 2026 data for CPA/apply rate benchmarks
